title: Fix GetFileFn proto-shape mismatch (F-4.6.1-2) + add agents/query/wiring.py real gRPC callables (Python-side half of F-4.6.1-1)

findings_forwarded.description: api/main.go's notImplementedPipeline is still unreplaced. Closing it requires a new proto/hivemind.proto RPC through which api/'s Go /query route can invoke agents/query/pipeline.py's run_query_pipeline (Python as gRPC server, mirroring ProposeSplit's reversed direction -- no such RPC exists today), regenerated Go+Python stubs, a runnable Python server process, and a real Go gRPC client replacing notImplementedPipeline in api/main.go.

test_query_e2e.py still only uses k=1; the topic-expansion branch (expand_insufficient_topics) remains untested end-to-end on-disk. Needs a k=2 (or otherwise expansion-triggering) e2e test extending test_query_e2e.py's real-corpus pattern.

For file_ids reachable only via GraphNeighbors expansion (never present in select_top_k's selected TopicCandidates), no message anywhere in proto/hivemind.proto (Neighbor, GetFileResponse) carries a path. pipeline.py's _build_selected_markdown now falls back to a disclosed placeholder header for these ids rather than fabricating a path. Closing this for good needs either a path field added to GetFileResponse/Neighbor, or a new path-lookup RPC -- out of scope for 4.6.3.1, forwarded alongside task-4.6.3.2.

self_consistency.environment_note: System/anaconda python has a pre-existing, unrelated protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch causing hivemind_pb2 import failures (reproduces identically on already-committed agents/ingestion/test_shortlist.py's grpc tests) -- not introduced by this run. Use agents/.venv/bin/python, not the anaconda python, to reproduce these results.

note_to_verifier: Per invariant I4, this agent did not verify its own work. Please verify: (1) GetFileFn's new shape and path_by_id sourcing in pipeline.py, (2) wiring.py's three Grpc*Client translations against proto/hivemind.proto's actual message shapes, (3) that test_pipeline.py's updated fixtures/assertions are not vacuous, (4) that api/ and proto/ are genuinely untouched (git diff scope), (5) the two forwarded findings above are legitimately out of scope for a single commit rather than corner-cutting.
